title: Strategy mining batch 2026-06-10-01
status_boundary: Research candidates only; none are production-ready or live-trading candidates.

selection_policy.batch_goal: Expand beyond the existing funding, basis, OI, orderbook and stablecoin backlog while keeping every candidate replayable with public market data or explicit manual gates.
selection_policy.promotion_rule: Promote only candidates with clear data requirements, cost terms, failure modes and operator fit; leave the rest as research batch backlog.

candidates.hypothesis: When perp mark price diverges from index price beyond a rolling threshold and then starts compressing, the window may identify liquidation stress, exchange-specific risk or a mean-reversion alert suitable for filtering carry and momentum signals.
candidates.rationale: Uses mark/index data already modeled by the project and can initially operate as alert or risk filter instead of direct execution.

candidates.hypothesis: Extreme funding, rising OI and stalled price continuation can mark crowded perp positioning; subsequent funding normalization or OI drop may produce an unwind alert.
candidates.rationale: Reuses funding and OI concepts but tests a distinct crowded-unwind failure mode for existing carry and momentum strategies.

candidates.hypothesis: A transition from compressed realized volatility to expanding realized volatility can improve whether carry, basis or momentum candidates are enabled, disabled or downgraded.
candidates.rationale: Useful as a portfolio-level filter and can be validated with trade-derived candles before adding new exchange-specific data models.

candidates.hypothesis: After premium index reaches an extreme and stops expanding, short holding-period compression may help classify whether a perp premium z-score signal is actionable.
candidates.rationale: Good follow-on to #7, but blocked until premium_index_kline is modeled and collected.

candidates.hypothesis: Among top liquid perps, relative strength with funding and OI crowding filters may separate broad beta from idiosyncratic continuation.
candidates.rationale: Useful but needs careful cross-sectional universe and rebalance cost controls.

candidates.hypothesis: Funding carry candidates may perform better when realized volatility and mark/index dislocation are below regime thresholds, and should be disabled during stress transitions.
candidates.rationale: Likely better expressed as an extension of funding_carry_vol_filter after the first replay is complete.

candidates.hypothesis: Large forced-move candles with high volume, OI contraction and mark/index dislocation may identify liquidation cascades that later mean revert.
candidates.rationale: Stress-window slippage and adverse selection are material; keep as alert until execution quality is quantified.

candidates.hypothesis: Within related crypto sectors, extreme dispersion between liquid perp returns may partially revert when the move is not confirmed by volume and OI.
candidates.rationale: Sector taxonomy and universe drift make it easy to overfit; keep for later.

candidates.hypothesis: Exchange changes to funding interval, cap, floor or calculation method can create temporary strategy invalidation windows that should suppress carry and unwind signals.
candidates.rationale: Better as manual risk gate than alpha strategy.

candidates.hypothesis: Dated futures basis may roll down nonlinearly near delivery; a days-to-expiry and liquidity filter may improve quarterly_basis_convergence exits.
candidates.rationale: Likely an extension of quarterly_basis_convergence after future metadata and depth blockers are resolved.
